title: ajuga salicifolia subsp. bassarabica
intro: Ajuga salicifolia subsp. bassarabica, also known as Bugloss or Bugloss Bugleweed, is a subspecies of Ajuga salicifolia, a plant native to the Balkan Peninsula. This perennial herb is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides and iridoids. The health benefits of Ajuga salicifolia subsp. bassarabica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
